sources.title: Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners, CR 25-16, Fishing Seasons and Regulations (Jan. 1, 2026 – Dec. 31, 2027) — Washoe County, Lake Tahoe and tributaries
sources.quotedText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries    Open year around except for closed areas    Daily: 5 game fish    Possession: 10 game fish    1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset

sources.title: Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners, CR 25-16 — Washoe County CLOSED WATERS (Second Creek not listed)
sources.quotedText: Third Creek and its tributaries from Lake Tahoe to the Mt. Rose Highway No. 431. Incline Creek and its tributaries from Lake Tahoe to Ski Way and the Mt. Rose Highway No. 431. Wood Creek from Lake Tahoe to the Mt. Rose Highway No. 431. Lake Tahoe within a 200-yard radius of the mouths of Third, Incline and Wood creeks. A 500-yard radius from Sand Harbor boat ramp.

sources.title: Nevada General Statewide Fishing Regulations — Fishing License Requirement (NDOW / eRegulations)
sources.quotedText: Any resident 12 years of age or older who fishes in Nevada is required to have a fishing license.

parameters.note: Second Creek flows through Incline Village directly into Lake Tahoe in Washoe County, Nevada, so it is a Lake Tahoe tributary governed by NDOW's Washoe County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing in CR 25-16: open year-round, except for closed areas. Notably, Second Creek is NOT one of the Washoe County closed waters — the CR 25-16 closed-waters list names only Third, Incline, and Wood creeks (each closed from Lake Tahoe up to Mt. Rose Highway) and 200-yard radii around the mouths of Third, Incline, and Wood; Second Creek and its mouth are omitted, so the general open-year-round tributary season applies to it with no stated upstream distance limit. This is the Nevada rule and differs from the California §7.50(b)(152) seasonal catch-and-release closure that applies to Tahoe tributaries on the California side of the lake.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Washoe Co. — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Open year-round as a Lake Tahoe tributary; Second Creek is not among the Washoe County closed creeks (Third, Incline, and Wood are).
regulations.verbatimText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Open year around except for closed areas.

parameters.counting_rule: The Washoe County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing sets 5 game fish daily and 10 in possession, with no species sub-limit. 'Game fish' in Nevada includes trout, mountain whitefish, kokanee salmon, bass, and other listed game species combined.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Washoe Co. — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: 5 game fish daily, 10 in possession (Lake Tahoe and tributaries listing; no species sub-limit)
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 5 game fish    Possession: 10 game fish

parameters.note: The Washoe County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing restricts fishing hours to 1 hour before sunrise through 2 hours after sunset (unlike most other Washoe waters, which are open any hour of the day or night).

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Washoe Co. — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.
regulations.verbatimText: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset

parameters.note: Nevada requires a fishing license for residents and nonresidents 12 years of age or older; anglers under 12 need no license (nonresidents under 12 are limited to 50% of the daily bag). The California/Nevada Lake Tahoe license reciprocity applies to fishing IN Lake Tahoe itself (and the Nevada Interstate Boundary Waters License is valid only on the lake), so it does NOT extend to Second Creek above the lake — a Nevada fishing license is required to fish this tributary.

regulations.humanSummary: Nevada fishing license required for anglers 12+ (lake license reciprocity does not extend to the tributary).
regulations.verbatimText: Any resident 12 years of age or older who fishes in Nevada is required to have a fishing license.
